articles.title: Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts
articles.description:
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth accused these elite institutions of "indoctrination" that favors "leftist ideology."

The post Citing ‘wokeness,’ Pentagon cancels military fellowships at New England schools like MIT and Tufts appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Pentagon cancels military fellowships at MIT and Tufts due to accusations of 'wokeness'.
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth cites 'indoctrination' favoring leftist ideology in elite institutions.
Local students and military personnel lose access to valuable fellowship opportunities starting March 2026.

articles.title: Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass.
articles.description:
The little bird is now receiving care “to get her tiny little self back in the air again,” wildlife rescuers said.

The post Rescued pocket-sized owl turning heads in Mass. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Wildlife rescuers are caring for a rescued pocket-sized owl in Massachusetts.
The owl is being rehabilitated to regain flight capabilities after its rescue.
Local residents are captivated by the owl's story, raising awareness for wildlife care.

articles.title: Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs
articles.description:
A narrow 10–7 vote ended GLP-1 coverage for state employees, citing high premium costs and a tight state budget.

The post Massachusetts joins insurers cutting coverage for popular weight-loss drugs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts ended GLP-1 coverage for state employees on a 10-7 vote.
Decision driven by high premium costs and a tight state budget.
Boston residents may face increased out-of-pocket expenses for weight-loss medications.

articles.title: After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms
articles.description:
The officials issued guidance after federal agents in New York reportedly accessed a Columbia building by falsely saying that they were looking for a missing child.

The post After Columbia incident, Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE needs a judicial warrant to enter dorms appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Healey and Campbell remind schools that ICE requires a judicial warrant for dorm access.
This guidance follows a reported incident at Columbia University involving federal agents.
Boston schools are urged to protect student privacy and rights amid federal enforcement.

articles.title: Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site
articles.description:
“We don't believe that there's anything to hide when it comes to American history.”

The post Lawsuit challenges National Park Service over content removal, including at a Mass. site appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lawsuit filed against National Park Service over content removal at Massachusetts historical site.
Plaintiffs argue transparency is essential for understanding American history and its complexities.
Potential changes could affect educational resources and historical narratives for Boston residents.
